pattern: Bitmask DP over which individuals have crossed, combined with the current cyclic stage, finds the minimum total boat time while accounting for mandatory return trips.
hints: State is (set of people on the destination side, current stage) — both are small enough for memoization since n≤12 and m≤5.
At each state choose a subset of remaining people (size ≤k) to send across; if any remain after, also choose who returns — pick whoever minimizes future time.
Crossing time is max(time[i] for i in group) × mul[stage]; stage advances by floor(crossing_time) % m. Return trips follow the same rule. If k≥n when people remain on one side, no return is needed.

edge_inputs.description: single person, trivially crosses in one trip
three people, two trips needed with return
k equals n, everyone crosses in one trip
stage multiplier > 1 slows return and forward equally
k=1 and n=1, trivially one trip

stress.description: random small instances within all constraint bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, min(size, 8))
    k = rng.randint(1, 5)
    m = rng.randint(1, 5)
    time_arr = [rng.randint(1, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    mul_arr = [round(rng.uniform(0.5, 2.0), 2) for _ in range(m)]
    return [n, k, m, time_arr, mul_arr]
stress.note: small n keeps search feasible for oracle
